title: Mulligatawny Soup

overview.description: A hearty Anglo-Indian soup combining chicken, red lentils, vegetables and warming spices in a rich homemade stock.

method: Heat the oil or ghee over a medium heat.
Brown the chicken for 4–5 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Cook the onion, carrots and celery for 8–10 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, turmeric, curry powder and black pepper.
Add the tomatoes, red lentils and chicken stock.
Return the chicken to the pan.
Bring to the boil, then simmer gently for 30 minutes until the lentils have broken down.
Blend until smooth, or leave partially blended if you prefer a little texture.
Stir in the lemon juice and season to taste.
Finish with fresh coriander before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Mulligatawny developed during the British Raj as an adaptation of South Indian pepper water to suit British tastes.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a lightly spiced soup using homemade stock, lentils and chicken, and how blending changes the finished texture.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A warming, filling soup that makes excellent use of homemade chicken stock and freezes beautifully.
chapman-tips: Homemade chicken stock gives this soup much more depth than stock cubes.
Blend until completely smooth for a classic finish, or only partially blend if you prefer more texture.
Add the lemon juice right at the end to brighten the flavours.
This is an Anglo-Indian recipe rather than a traditional Indian soup.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently and loosen with a little stock if it thickens.
